query:
INSERT INTO devotionals (title, verse, verse_reference, content, prayer, published_at, created_at, updated_at) 
VALUES ('Trusting God in Difficult Times', 'For we walk by faith, not by sight.', '2 Corinthians 5:7', 'When life feels overwhelming and the path ahead seems unclear, it''s easy to doubt God''s presence. Yet faith isn''t about understanding everything—it''s about trusting the One who does.

During difficult seasons, we often wish we could see the outcome before taking the next step. But God invites us to walk by faith, not by sight. This means trusting His character, His promises, and His timing even when circumstances suggest otherwise.

Consider the Israelites in the wilderness. They couldn''t see the promised land, yet God led them faithfully. Their journey teaches us that faith isn''t passive—it''s an active choice to believe God''s goodness despite our circumstances.

When you face uncertainty today, remember: God hasn''t abandoned you. He walks with you through every valley. Your faith doesn''t need to be perfect; it just needs to be real. Take one step forward, trusting that God sees what you cannot.', 'Lord, help me trust You even when I cannot see the way forward. Strengthen my faith and remind me of Your faithfulness. Amen.', NOW(), NOW(), NOW());

INSERT INTO devotionals (title, verse, verse_reference, content, prayer, published_at, created_at, updated_at) 
VALUES ('The Power of Prayer', 'Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God.', 'Philippians 4:6', 'Prayer is not a last resort—it''s our first line of defense against anxiety, fear, and despair. Yet many of us treat prayer as something we do when all else fails, rather than as the foundation of our daily lives.

God invites us to bring everything to Him in prayer. Not just our big concerns, but our worries, frustrations, and uncertainties. When we pray with thanksgiving, acknowledging God''s past faithfulness, we shift our perspective from what we lack to what we''ve been given.

The promise in Philippians is remarkable: when we pray, God''s peace—a peace that surpasses all understanding—guards our hearts and minds. This isn''t the absence of problems; it''s the presence of God''s comfort in the midst of them.

Start today by bringing one concern to God in prayer. Thank Him for His faithfulness in the past. Watch how His peace begins to settle over your situation.', 'Father, teach me to pray with confidence and thanksgiving. Guard my heart and mind with Your peace. Amen.', NOW(), NOW(), NOW());

INSERT INTO devotionals (title, verse, verse_reference, content, prayer, published_at, created_at, updated_at) 
VALUES ('God''s Grace is Sufficient', 'My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.', '2 Corinthians 12:9', 'We live in a culture that celebrates strength, independence, and self-sufficiency. Yet God''s economy works differently. His grace flows most abundantly into our weakness.

Paul discovered this truth when he asked God to remove a thorn from his flesh. Instead of removing it, God said, "My grace is sufficient for you." This wasn''t a rejection of Paul''s request—it was an invitation to experience God''s power in a deeper way.

Our weaknesses become windows through which God''s strength shines. When we acknowledge our limitations and turn to Him, we discover that His grace is not just enough—it''s abundant, renewing, and transformative.

What weakness are you struggling with today? Instead of trying to overcome it alone, invite God into it. Let His grace be sufficient. You''ll find that His power works best when we stop relying on our own.', 'Lord, help me embrace my weakness and trust in Your sufficient grace. Show me Your power in my limitations. Amen.', NOW(), NOW(), NOW());

INSERT INTO devotionals (title, verse, verse_reference, content, prayer, published_at, created_at, updated_at) 
VALUES ('Strength Through Christ', 'I can do all this through him who gives me strength.', 'Philippians 4:13', 'There are moments in life when we feel completely inadequate. The task seems too big, the challenge too difficult, the burden too heavy. In these moments, we need to remember: our strength doesn''t come from within ourselves—it comes from Christ.

This verse isn''t a promise that we''ll never face difficulties or that everything will be easy. Rather, it''s a declaration that Christ''s strength is available to us in every circumstance. When we''re weak, He is strong. When we''re afraid, He is courageous. When we''re lost, He is our guide.

The key is connection. We access Christ''s strength through prayer, through His Word, through community, and through surrender. When we stop trying to do everything in our own power and instead lean on Him, we discover a strength that transcends our circumstances.

What challenge are you facing that feels impossible? Bring it to Christ. Ask Him for strength. Then step forward, not in your own power, but in His.', 'Christ, I surrender my weakness to Your strength. Help me trust in Your power today. Amen.', NOW(), NOW(), NOW());

INSERT INTO devotionals (title, verse, verse_reference, content, prayer, published_at, created_at, updated_at) 
VALUES ('Overcoming Fear with Faith', 'For God has not given us a spirit of fear, but of power, love, and a sound mind.', '2 Timothy 1:7', 'Fear is a powerful emotion that can paralyze us, preventing us from pursuing God''s calling and living fully. Yet God doesn''t want us to live in fear. He offers us something better: power, love, and a sound mind.

Fear often whispers lies: "You''re not good enough." "You''ll fail." "God has abandoned you." But these aren''t God''s voice. God speaks truth. He reminds us of our worth, our purpose, and His faithfulness.

When we encounter fear, we have a choice. We can believe the lie, or we can believe God''s truth. We can shrink back, or we can step forward in faith. We can let fear control us, or we can let love cast it out.

Identify one fear that''s holding you back today. Now speak God''s truth to it. Remind yourself of His power, His love for you, and the sound mind He''s given you. Step forward, not without fear, but despite it.', 'Lord, replace my fear with Your power, love, and sound mind. Help me step forward in faith. Amen.', NOW(), NOW(), NOW());

INSERT INTO devotionals (title, verse, verse_reference, content, prayer, published_at, created_at, updated_at) 
VALUES ('Staying Consistent in Prayer', 'Pray without ceasing.', '1 Thessalonians 5:17', '"Pray without ceasing" sounds impossible in our busy, distracted world. Yet this invitation isn''t about spending every moment on your knees. It''s about maintaining a constant awareness of God''s presence and bringing Him into every part of your day.

Consistent prayer isn''t about perfect prayers or lengthy devotions. It''s about showing up, day after day, with an open heart. It''s about the quick prayer while driving, the gratitude whispered while cooking, the honest cry for help in a difficult moment.

When we pray consistently, we develop intimacy with God. We begin to see His hand in our daily lives. We notice His guidance, His comfort, His provision. Prayer becomes not a task on our to-do list, but a natural rhythm of our relationship with Him.

Start small. Commit to one consistent prayer practice this week. Perhaps it''s a morning prayer, a mealtime blessing, or a bedtime reflection. Let this practice anchor you to God''s presence.', 'Father, help me develop a consistent prayer life. Remind me to seek You throughout my day. Amen.', NOW(), NOW(), NOW());

INSERT INTO devotionals (title, verse, verse_reference, content, prayer, published_at, created_at, updated_at) 
VALUES ('God''s Timing is Perfect', 'There is a time for everything, and a season for every activity under the heavens.', 'Ecclesiastes 3:1', 'We live in an age of instant gratification. We want answers now, results immediately, and solutions without delay. Yet God operates on a different timeline. His timing is always perfect, even when it doesn''t match our expectations.

Abraham waited decades for the son God promised. David waited years before becoming king. Jesus waited thirty years before beginning His ministry. These weren''t delays—they were preparations. God was working behind the scenes, developing character, building faith, and preparing the way.

When we''re waiting for God''s promises to come to fruition, it''s easy to doubt. But waiting is not wasted time. It''s a season of growth, of deepening trust, of learning to rely on God rather than on our circumstances.

What are you waiting for? Instead of anxiously trying to force an outcome, surrender your timeline to God. Trust that He''s working even in the waiting. His timing is always perfect.', 'Lord, help me trust Your timing. Give me patience as I wait for Your promises. Amen.', NOW(), NOW(), NOW());

INSERT INTO devotionals (title, verse, verse_reference, content, prayer, published_at, created_at, updated_at) 
VALUES ('Living with Purpose', 'For we are God''s handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do.', 'Ephesians 2:10', 'Every person has been created with purpose. You''re not here by accident. God has a plan for your life, and He''s prepared specific good works for you to do.

Many of us spend our lives searching for purpose, wondering if we matter, questioning whether our lives make a difference. Yet the Bible declares that we are God''s handiwork—His masterpiece. We were created with intention, and we''ve been equipped with gifts and talents to fulfill our calling.

Your purpose might not be grand or public. It might be loving your family faithfully, serving your community, being a faithful friend, or using your skills to help others. Whatever it is, it matters. It''s part of God''s plan.

Reflect on your gifts and passions. How might God be calling you to use them? What good works has He prepared for you? Don''t wait for perfect clarity. Take one step forward in faith, trusting that God will guide you.', 'God, help me discover and live out the purpose You''ve created me for. Guide me to the good works You''ve prepared. Amen.', NOW(), NOW(), NOW());

INSERT INTO devotionals (title, verse, verse_reference, content, prayer, published_at, created_at, updated_at) 
VALUES ('The Importance of Forgiveness', 'Bear with each other and forgive one another if any of you has a grievance against someone. Forgive as the Lord forgave you.', 'Colossians 3:13', 'Forgiveness is one of the most difficult yet transformative acts we can perform. When someone hurts us, our natural response is to hold onto the hurt, to demand justice, to make them pay. Yet Jesus calls us to something radical: forgive as God has forgiven us.

God''s forgiveness is complete and unconditional. Through Christ, He has forgiven all our sins—past, present, and future. He doesn''t hold our failures against us. He doesn''t demand payment or punishment. He simply forgives and restores us to relationship with Him.

When we forgive others, we''re not saying what they did was okay. We''re not excusing their behavior or avoiding justice. We''re releasing them from our judgment and entrusting them to God. More importantly, we''re freeing ourselves from the burden of bitterness.

Is there someone you need to forgive? Forgiveness doesn''t require them to apologize or change. It''s a gift you give yourself—and a reflection of God''s forgiveness toward you.', 'Lord, help me forgive as You have forgiven me. Free me from bitterness and restore my relationships. Amen.', NOW(), NOW(), NOW());

INSERT INTO devotionals (title, verse, verse_reference, content, prayer, published_at, created_at, updated_at) 
VALUES ('Walking in God''s Light', 'I have come into the world as a light, so that no one who believes in me should stay in darkness.', 'John 12:46', 'Darkness can be overwhelming. It might be the darkness of depression, despair, confusion, or sin. In these moments, it''s easy to feel lost and alone. Yet Jesus promises that He is the light of the world, and He invites us to walk in His light.

Walking in God''s light means living in truth, in hope, and in the knowledge of His love. It means allowing His Word to illuminate our path and His Spirit to guide our steps. It means choosing faith over fear, love over hate, and forgiveness over bitterness.

When we walk in God''s light, we''re not just saved from darkness—we become light for others. Our lives become a testimony to God''s goodness and grace. We become beacons of hope in a dark world.

Where are you experiencing darkness today? Invite Jesus into that darkness. Ask Him to be your light. Then step forward, trusting that He will illuminate your path.', 'Jesus, be my light in the darkness. Guide me into truth and hope. Help me shine Your light for others. Amen.', NOW(), NOW(), NOW());

INSERT INTO devotionals (title, verse, verse_reference, content, prayer, published_at, created_at, updated_at) 
VALUES ('Rest in God''s Love', 'Come to me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.', 'Matthew 11:28', 'In our fast-paced world, rest has become a luxury rather than a necessity. We push ourselves harder, do more, achieve more, yet find ourselves increasingly exhausted and empty. Jesus offers a different way: come to Him, and find rest.

This rest isn''t just physical. It''s spiritual, emotional, and mental. It''s the peace that comes from knowing you''re loved unconditionally, that you don''t have to earn God''s approval, that your worth isn''t determined by your productivity.

When we come to Jesus with our burdens—our worries, our failures, our shame—He doesn''t judge us. He welcomes us. He offers us rest and restoration. He reminds us that we''re more than what we do, and we''re deeply loved for who we are.

What burden are you carrying today? Bring it to Jesus. Rest in His love. Let Him restore your soul.', 'Jesus, I come to You weary and burdened. Give me rest and restore my soul in Your love. Amen.', NOW(), NOW(), NOW());
